mechanism_narrative: An always-on rise in CPIAUCSL over the prior three months raises expected policy restraint and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 288.663 for 2022-04-01, available before 2022-05-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month CPIAUCSL growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market prices faster discount-rate increases and lower future margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25pp over 3 months while CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.33 for 2022-04-01 before the decision date
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-trend inflation
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have mean 20-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional mean, or if the negative hit rate is below 45% across two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in UNRATE signals weakening household income and rising recession risk, which reduces expected earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value was 3.6 for 2022-04-01 before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-triggered episodes do not show lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered episodes at p < 0.10, or if triggered positive-return frequency exceeds 65%.

mechanism_narrative: Falling RSAFS after adjusting for concurrent CPIAUCSL pressure signals real consumption deterioration, lowering revenue expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is negative while CPIAUCSL 3-month growth is positive
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 667724.0 for 2022-03-01 before the decision date
regime_hint: inflation squeeze on household real purchasing power
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction trigger has nonnegative average 20-day excess return versus all dates in walk-forward testing, or if its Rank IC is greater than -0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An always-on increase in INDPRO over the prior three months indicates expanding output and operating leverage, supporting earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value was 104.5853 for 2022-03-01 before the decision date
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with positive production momentum
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum is not associated with above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is below 0.03 in more than half of windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, leading investors to price lower cyclical earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 1793.0 for 2022-03-01 before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside-trigger episodes produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than the unconditional sample average, or if negative-return frequency is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth with low UNRATE indicates durable labor income without immediate recession stress, supporting consumption and earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE is at or below 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 151314.0 and UNRATE was 3.6 for 2022-04-01 before the decision date
regime_hint: tight labor market expansion before unemployment deterioration
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction trigger produces positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of observations or if average triggered return is below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising and INDPRO is falling, the economy exhibits stagflationary pressure that combines margin compression with weaker output, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month growth is positive while INDPRO 3-month growth is negative
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL and INDPRO vintages for early 2022 were available before 2022-05-15
regime_hint: stagflation scare with inflation pressure and production weakness
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation observations have median 20-day S&P 500 returns above zero, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward logistic tests for return direction.
